ddls:
CREATE TABLE "performance_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance log entry */,
  "starttime" TEXT /* Timestamp when the performance measurement started */,
  "seconds" INTEGER /* Total time in seconds for the process */,
  "ctime" INTEGER /* CPU time used by the process */,
  "dtime" INTEGER /* Disk time used by the process */,
  "ttime" INTEGER /* Total time taken for the process */,
  "wait" INTEGER /* Wait time during the process execution */,
  "app_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the application associated with the log */,
  "server_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the server where the application is running */,
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the specific metric being tracked */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status indicating the health of the application */,
  "details" TEXT /* Additional details or context about the log entry */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_logs_server_id FOREIGN KEY ("server_id") REFERENCES servers ("server_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_logs_metric_id FOREIGN KEY ("metric_id") REFERENCES metrics ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_logs_app_id FOREIGN KEY ("app_id") REFERENCES applications ("app_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "applications" (
  "app_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each application */,
  "app_name" TEXT /* Name of the application */,
  "app_version" TEXT /* Version of the application */,
  "environment" TEXT /* Deployment environment (e.g., production, staging) */,
  "owner_id" INTEGER /* ID of the owner responsible for the application */,
  "server_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the server where the application is deployed */,
  "last_deployed" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last deployment */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status indicating the current state of the application */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the application */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("app_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_applications_owner_id FOREIGN KEY ("owner_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_applications_server_id FOREIGN KEY ("server_id") REFERENCES servers ("server_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username used for logging into the system */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, developer, analyst) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for user authentication */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last login */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the user account is active */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the user account was created */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the performance log that triggered the alert */,
  "app_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the application associated with the alert */,
  "alert_type" TEXT /* Type of alert (e.g., High CPU Usage, Low Memory) */,
  "alert_message" TEXT /* Detailed message describing the alert */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the alert was generated */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user who created the alert */,
  "resolved_by" INTEGER /* Reference to the user who resolved the alert */,
  "resolved_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the alert was resolved */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status indicating the current state of the alert */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_resolved_by FOREIGN KEY ("resolved_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_app_id FOREIGN KEY ("app_id") REFERENCES applications ("app_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_log_id FOREIGN KEY ("log_id") REFERENCES performance_logs ("log_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each metric */,
  "metric_name" TEXT /* Name of the performance metric */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the metric */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for the metric */,
  "threshold" INTEGER /* Threshold value for the metric */,
  "is_critical" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the metric is critical */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the metric was created */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Permissions granted to the role (comma-separated) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the role was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the role */,
  "is_default" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the role is a default role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "servers" (
  "server_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each server */,
  "server_name" TEXT /* Name of the server */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the server */,
  "os" TEXT /* Operating system of the server */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the server */,
  "last_reboot" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last reboot */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status indicating the current state of the server */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("server_id")
)
